refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and safety zones
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation supporting dynamic updates and integration with planning modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data and operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and policy overlays, the design supports independence and clear interfaces, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data for model generation
Risk that operational policies may frequently change requiring flexible update mechanisms
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the generated model in agreed formats
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the need for modular separation between terrain representation and policy overlays to ensure maintainability and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental and operational data to create a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policy overlays.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and sensor data relevant to the worksite environment.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational policies and task directives to overlay on the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with other system modules and external data sources.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the module is designed to handle environmental and operational data integration; minimal diffusion as most data processing is internal.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on mobility and control, not data modeling; high diffusion as data would need to be shared externally.
Moderate containment for operational policy overlays but lacks terrain data processing; moderate diffusion due to reliance on external data.
Poor containment as safety services focus on alerts and monitoring, not data modeling; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it is specifically designed to integrate and model environmental and operational data, providing a cohesive and centralized implementation of the modular worksite model.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require frequent interface updates.
Integration complexity with operational policy overlays could increase coupling with Tasking interface.
Changes in terrain data formats might necessitate module revisions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically satisfies the FR by specifying a modular worksite model that integrates terrain data with operational policy overlays, directly addressing the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as the DP is primarily contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high containment and low diffusion scores.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is well-suited to contain the DP due to its design focus on integrating environmental and operational data. Other modules show low containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the availability of the generated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions, as well as validation against the agreed set of site features and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces for receiving terrain data, operational policies, and network communication.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on real-time data feeds to minimize interface update frequency.
Plan for integration complexity with operational policy overlays to reduce coupling with the Tasking interface.
Establish procedures to handle changes in terrain data formats to limit module revisions.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and minimal diffusion risks. The DP logically satisfies the FR, the NGHE Solution module is an appropriate and well-contained implementation boundary, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary decomposition that could increase complexity and risk. Recommended revisions focus on managing dependencies and integration complexity rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: Monitor and manage dependencies on real-time data feeds and integration complexity to reduce coupling and limit module revisions, ensuring robustness in the implementation module.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively contain the DP with minimal diffusion risk.
Verification methods for the worksite model/map are reliable and accessible.
Operational policies and terrain data interfaces are stable enough for current allocation.
open_risks: Potential changes in terrain data formats impacting module revisions.
Integration complexity with operational policy overlays may increase coupling.
Dependency on real-time data feeds could affect interface stability.
evidence_needed: Validation results of the worksite model/map against site features and policies.
Monitoring reports on interface update frequency and dependency management.
Assessment of integration complexity impact on module coupling.
